description: A designed graphic built from a screenshot of a CNN article header that has been deliberately edited/marked up to recast the framing of an immigration story. The image reproduces CNN's web layout: the CNN logo, hamburger menu and a red 'Subscribe' button at top, a 'US | 3 MIN READ' kicker, the byline 'By Taylor Romine' with a CNN avatar, and '22 HR AGO' timestamp. The black headline text on white reads 'ICE ... in Maryland who ... attempted to ram them with his van'. Several phrases have been overlaid with bright red highlight boxes containing white text inserted to change the meaning: 'officers defended themselves', 'from a criminal illegal alien', and a red block redacting/replacing original wording before 'attempted to ram them'. This is the 'fixed the headline for you' meme/propaganda technique (per the tweet 'Fixed the headline for you, @CNN.') used to accuse mainstream media of bias and to reframe the incident in ICE's favor by emphasizing self-defense and the suspect's immigration/criminal status. Target audience: supporters skeptical of mainstream media.
notable_text: CNN US; ICE officers defended themselves from a criminal illegal alien in Maryland who attempted to ram them with his van; By Taylor Romine; 22 HR AGO
